topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet afternoon. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. It was a request from Mark. Mark, the one who used to make my lunchbox contents a source of whispered dread in the third grade. My thumb hovered over the 'Accept' button, the plastic cool against my skin. I glanced around my room, suddenly hyper-aware of the shadows stretching long across the carpet. A strange tightness gripped my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. What was he thinking? The silence in the room seemed to amplify the rapid drumming of my pulse.

[story 2]

I stared at the profile picture—a slightly too-confident smirk I remembered vividly. A wave of apprehension washed over me, making my hands tremble slightly as I navigated to the message section. Every muscle in my jaw felt tight. I reread the request name three times, searching for some hidden clue, some reason this contact would suddenly materialize. When I finally typed a hesitant reply, the words felt clumsy and inadequate. I immediately minimized the chat window, leaning back in my chair, my gaze fixed on the closed door. A prickling sensation ran down my arms, and I instinctively pulled my sweater tighter around myself, as if trying to physically shield myself from whatever this interaction might entail.

[story 3]

The sound of the incoming request felt like a sudden, sharp intake of air. I dropped my phone onto the desk with a soft thud, the resulting silence immediately feeling heavy and suffocating. My palms grew slick. I couldn't look away from the screen; the familiar face, now presented as a digital connection, brought a rush of unwelcome memory. I took a slow, deliberate sip of water, trying to steady the frantic rhythm of my breathing. *Why now?* The question echoed in my mind, sharp and insistent. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between my bed and the window, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. The air in the room felt suddenly thin.
